document.name: microservice-dev-session
document.title: Microservice dev-session: provision a live dev preview, then hand off to an interactive coding agent
document.summary: Generalizes the per-run live-sync loop to ANY microservice (workflow-builder/workflow-orchestrator/function-router). Provisions that service's per-run dev preview (kept alive, browsable at its tailnet URL), clones the repo into the shared /sandbox/work + writes a sync.sh helper, then ENDS INTO a persistent interactive coding-agent session bound to the same workspace + preview. Keep prompting the agent: it edits repo/, runs ./sync.sh to live-sync, you inspect. P3 of the agentic dev loop (docs/agentic-deploy-inspect-loop.md).

service.title: Microservice to develop
service.description: workflow-builder | workflow-orchestrator | function-router (registered in the dev-preview registry).

mode.title: Dev-preview mode
mode.description: host-throwaway (legacy per-run preview on the host dev cluster) | preview-native (run INSIDE a Tier-2 vcluster preview: REPLACE the preview's prod pod with this dev server + adopt its Service, so the preview's own URL serves live edits). preview-native is for workflow-builder (the BFF) first.

keepPreview.title: Keep the preview alive after the run

artifacts.from: ${ { service: (.data.service // ""), url: (.data.url // ""), browseUrl: (.data.browseUrl // ""), syncUrl: (.data.syncUrl // ""), podIP: (.data.podIP // ""), ready: (.data.ready // false) } }
artifacts.title: Per-run dev preview

with.command:
${ "export REPOURL=" + ((.trigger.repoUrl // "PittampalliOrg/workflow-builder") | @sh) + "; export SUBDIR=" + ((.provision_preview.repoSubdir // ".") | @sh) + "; export PATHS=" + (((.provision_preview.syncPaths // ["src"]) | join(" ")) | @sh) + "; export SYNCURL=" + ((.provision_preview.syncUrl // "") | @sh) + ";" + "
set -e
cd /sandbox/work
rm -rf repo
git clone --depth 1 --single-branch https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/$REPOURL.git repo
printf 'SUBDIR=%s\nPATHS="%s"\nSYNCURL=%s\n' "$SUBDIR" "$PATHS" "$SYNCURL" > /sandbox/work/.syncenv
cat > /sandbox/work/sync.sh <<'SH'
#!/bin/sh
set -e
. /sandbox/work/.syncenv
cd /sandbox/work/repo/$SUBDIR
SYNCPATHS=""; for p in $PATHS; do [ -e "$p" ] && SYNCPATHS="$SYNCPATHS $p"; done
tar -czf /tmp/sync.tgz $SYNCPATHS
curl -sS -X POST --data-binary @/tmp/sync.tgz -H 'content-type: application/gzip' "$SYNCURL"
echo
echo SYNCED
SH
chmod +x /sandbox/work/sync.sh
echo CLONED $(git -C repo rev-parse --short HEAD); echo '--- .syncenv ---'; cat /sandbox/work/.syncenv" }

with.instructions:
${ "You are an interactive developer for the microservice **" + (.trigger.service // "workflow-orchestrator") + "**.

- The repo is cloned at /sandbox/work/repo. EDIT FILES UNDER /sandbox/work/repo/" + (.provision_preview.repoSubdir // ".") + " (only paths under there are synced).
- A live dev preview of this service is ALREADY running with hot-reload.
- After you edit files, run /sandbox/work/sync.sh to push your changes to the preview; it hot-reloads in a few seconds.
- VERIFY from inside this sandbox by curling the in-cluster preview URL " + (.provision_preview.url // "") + " (e.g. curl " + (.provision_preview.url // "") + "/<your-route>). Do NOT curl the public URL " + (.provision_preview.browseUrl // "") + " from here — it is for the human to open in a browser and is NOT resolvable inside the cluster.
- The in-cluster sync endpoint is " + (.provision_preview.syncUrl // "") + " (sync.sh targets it).

Reply 'ready' and then wait for the user's next message describing the change to make. Then implement it under repo/" + (.provision_preview.repoSubdir // ".") + ", run /sandbox/work/sync.sh, and confirm it is live by curling the in-cluster preview URL." }
